nodes.label: segmented-control supersession — record Kim's T3 ruling (2026-07-07: the tag IS the requirement) as ADR-0095 built from ADR-0092 §Alternatives-1, and plan the promotion wave
Phase A — the design/documentation pass (THIS dispatch; no source touched)
ADR-0095 authored from the recorded §Alternatives-1 design, cites the fired T3 trigger, re-verified file:line facts
nodes.accept: python ~/.claude/skills/adr-author/scripts/adr_check.py .claude/docs/adr/0095-*.md exits 0; Status is the bare 'proposed'; the Context names T3 as the fired reopen trigger per ADR-0092 clause 4 / its Acceptance standing rule

nodes.label: ADR-0092 disposition — superseded (overtaken unratified per its own §Consequences T3 branch), forward-linked, record preserved
nodes.accept: 0092's Status cell is the bare 'superseded'; its Supersedes/Superseded-by row names ADR-0095; git diff shows metadata/date-trail lines only — Context/Decision/Consequences/Alternatives untouched

nodes.label: ADR-0086 reciprocal back-link (Superseded by ADR-0095, pending its ratification — the 0016←0087 in-flight precedent; Status stays 'accepted' until the ratifier flips it)
nodes.accept: 0086's Supersedes/Superseded-by row names ADR-0095 with the flip-on-ratification note; the Status cell still reads the bare 'accepted'; Decision substance untouched (append-only)

nodes.label: README index truth — the 0086 row notes the pending supersession, the 0092 row flips to superseded, a 0095 row lands
nodes.accept: grep '0095' README.md hits the new row; the 0092 row's status column reads superseded; the 0086 row names ADR-0095

nodes.label: handoff block to the coordinator, doc-reviewer named as the independent critic before Kim ratifies
nodes.accept: the final report carries all eight handoff-compose fields in order, Status on line 1, every gate named by command with verdict or UNMEASURED

nodes.label: Phase B — the promotion build wave (SEPARATE follow-up dispatch, gated on 0095's ratification; ~1.5–2 days, component-builder + a2ui-builder + site seats)

UISegmentedControlElement extends UIRadioGroupElement + the parent's protected post-selection hook (variant retired from groupProps; orientation stays; #writeIndexCount moves down as the --ui-segmented-control-* writer, fired on commit AND formReset)
nodes.accept: radio-group's non-segmented jsdom suite green unmodified; a jsdom leg proves the hook fires on commit and on form reset with the renamed --ui-segmented-control-index/-count; radio-group.ts has no 'variant' prop and no #writeIndexCount

nodes.label: NEW ui-segment extends UIRadioElement — own descriptor + own CSS (no dot pseudo; its own ADR-0009 focus ring); #radios()'s instanceof walk matches by construction
nodes.accept: browser legs (both engines): segment renders no dot ::before, carries the fleet :focus-visible outline, role=radio + aria-checked exposed; radio.css untouched (git diff)

nodes.label: CSS move + re-key — the radio-group.css segmented blocks land in segmented-control.css keyed ui-segmented-control/ui-segment, tokens renamed --ui-segmented-control-*; ADR-0086's clauses 3–8 rendering contract carried over byte-equivalent
nodes.accept: the re-keyed browser acceptance (0086's list re-targeted: grid equal cells, ::before indicator transform math, motion + reduced-motion, forced-colors inversion, interaction states, per-orientation keyboard) green both engines; grep -r 'ui-radio-group-index\|ui-radio-group-count' src/ returns nothing

nodes.label: fleet surface — descriptors segmented-control.md/segment.md, barrel + package.json exports entries, ADR-0080 leave-one-out marginal pin, gallery member (descriptor-derived, the T3 payoff), family-coherence + attributes trip-wires
nodes.accept: npm test green incl. family-coherence.test.ts, barrels.test.ts, the attributes[]↔props trip-wire; npm run size (manual, ADR-0040 §3) shows the pinned marginal within cap; the gallery page lists ui-segmented-control

nodes.label: a2ui wire — NEW SegmentedControl catalog type + factory + conformance + derived-prompt inventory; RadioGroup.variant enum member retired (catalog.json:233); corpus re-checked for segmented exemplars
nodes.accept: catalog/factories/index + conformance + prompt-drift gates green; grep 'segmented' catalog.json hits only the SegmentedControl type, not a RadioGroup variant enum member

nodes.label: site migration — component-preview knob builder emits <ui-segmented-control>/<ui-segment>; both segmented browser suites re-keyed; radio-group.md variant rows/prose removed (orientation + per-orientation keyboard table STAND); dimensions.css:76 doctrine comment re-pointed
nodes.accept: component-preview segmented browser suite (re-keyed) green both engines; radio-group.md has no variant row but keeps orientation; dimensions.css:76 names the segmented-control indicator

nodes.label: single-wave hard cutover — new control + variant retirement in ONE change; zero-survivor greps; ADR-0086 status flip + log rows on ratification
nodes.accept: zero-survivor greps empty outside .claude/docs history: variant="segmented"/variant='segmented', --ui-radio-group-index/-count, the catalog enum member; npm run check && npm test green; both-engine browser run green; 0086's Status cell flipped to the bare 'superseded' by the ratifier

actions.label: record the T3 ruling as an ordered supersession that cites its fired trigger
preserve the overtaken proposal (0092) as history — disposition without deletion or substance edits
keep the log's two-way links and index truthful across all three records
hold lifecycle discipline — no self-ratification; the accepted ADR's status flip belongs to the ratifier
route the outcome for independent review (generator ≠ critic) and Kim's ratification
give the control a first-class tag identity (the substance of the ruling)
keep 100% of the shipped single-select behavior in ONE home — no forked machinery, no drift
keep the token naming law true (--ui-{name}-* owned by the named control)
keep consumer markup radio-free inside the segmented control (the naming win T3 is about)
keep rendering byte-equivalent — the supersession changes identity, not design
retire the old spelling everywhere — one spelling, no alias, no dual-support window
keep the a2ui wire vocabulary one-type-per-control and drift-gated
keep the fleet's standing gates green (descriptor/gallery/exports/marginal/coherence)
keep radio-group's surviving surface honest (orientation stays; its docs stay true)

edges.why: 0092's forward link names the new ADR's number — the record must exist before the pointer does
0086's back-link names ADR-0095 — same number dependency
the index row cites the authored ADR's path/title/repairs
the handoff reports the three records' final states — composed only after all edits land
same — the handoff routes on the completed lifecycle bookkeeping
same — the index rows are part of what the reviewer verifies
the build executes the ratified ADR's plan — Phase B is gated on 0095's ratification, not just its text
the CSS token rename consumes the hook's renamed --ui-segmented-control-* writes — the seam must exist first
the re-keyed selectors target ui-segment — the child tag must exist for the compound rules
descriptors/exports/gallery describe the real registered class + props
segment.md + the attributes trip-wire read the real segment props
the a2ui factory constructs the shipped element — the class lands before the catalog type
the site knob builder + re-keyed suites assert the moved CSS's rendered truth
the cutover gate runs the whole fleet surface — it integrates after the per-slice gates
the zero-survivor grep includes the catalog enum retirement
the migration must be complete before the zero-survivor grep can pass
